name: Spree Commerce
what_it_does: Open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce platform with REST API and GraphQL support for headless commerce applications.
use_cases: Build headless storefronts via Spree REST API for product catalog, cart, and checkout
Automate order management workflows including fulfillment, returns, and inventory updates
Integrate AI product recommendations by querying taxon and product associations
Manage multi-store deployments from a single Spree instance via the multi-store API
Build B2B platforms using Spree's custom pricing and customer group features
not_for: Teams without Ruby/Rails expertise — Spree customization requires Rails knowledge
High-scale storefronts that need enterprise SaaS reliability without infrastructure management
Projects where Shopify or BigCommerce managed hosting would reduce operational burden
best_when: Best for Ruby/Rails teams wanting a self-hosted, fully customizable open-source commerce platform.
avoid_when: Avoid when your team lacks Rails expertise or needs a zero-ops managed commerce solution.

auth.notes: Spree OAuth2 for storefront and admin tokens. Bearer tokens used in Authorization header.

pricing.paid_tiers: Spree Commerce Inc offers enterprise support
pricing.notes: AGPL licensed (v4+). Commercial license available for closed-source modifications.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — implement via Rack::Attack or reverse proxy

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses vary between REST and GraphQL endpoints; not always consistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cart and order operations are not idempotent — duplicate requests create duplicate records.
known_agent_gotchas: Spree 4.x has significant breaking changes from 3.x — check version before using API docs
Guest cart tokens expire — agents must persist and re-associate carts when token expires
Product variants vs products are separate resources — price and inventory live on variants, not products
Multi-store setup requires store-specific API tokens and different base URLs per store
AGPL license requires open-sourcing customizations — use commercial license for proprietary modifications

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 implemented but scope documentation is sparse. Self-hosted security depends on deployment.
